title: Normal -> Insert -> Normal
summary: The mode transition every editing change goes through.

frames.caption: Normal mode. Status line shows nothing — the default.
frames.narration: When you open a file, you start in Normal mode. The cursor is a block; keystrokes are commands, not input.

frames.caption: After i — Insert mode. '-- INSERT --' appears.
frames.narration: i enters Insert mode at the cursor. The status line below changes to advertise the new mode. Keystrokes now produce text.

frames.caption: After typing ', vim'.
frames.narration: Five characters appear at the cursor. The buffer is now modified — a [+] marker would appear in the file's status block in some configs.

frames.caption: After Esc — back to Normal mode.
frames.narration: Esc commits the insert and returns to Normal mode. The '-- INSERT --' indicator disappears. Vim records the entire insert as one undoable change.
